data.title: Teva agrees to pay $425M to settle Copaxone kickback case: DOJ
data.description:
• Teva (NYSE:TEVA) has agreed to pay $425M to resolve allegations that it paid kickbacks related to its multiple sclerosis drug Copaxone through two co-pay assistance foundations.
  • In its complaint, the U.S. government had accused TEVA of violating the Anti-Kickback Statute and causing the submission of false Medicare claims by manipulating the co-pay foundation assistance system, according to a statement by the U.S. Attorney’s Office in Massachusetts.
  • More specifically, the government alleged that from 2006 to 2017, Teva conspired with third parties that included a specialty pharmacy and two co-pay assistance foundations to direct its “supposed charitable payments” to patients taking Copaxone, while simultaneously raising the price of the drug, the statement said.
  • The U.S. Attorney’s Office said the settlement was reached “after the government’s review of Teva’s financial disclosures concerning its financial condition.”

data.title: Mandalay Resources announces cash balance for Q3 2024
data.description:
• Mandalay Resources (TSX:MND:CA) on Thursday announced that its Q3 quarter-end cash balance was $55M.
  • Consolidated gold equivalent production of 20,323 ounces for Q3.
  • MNDJF closed +2.08333% at $2.45
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Definity Financial releases estimate of financial impact from catastrophe losses for Q3
data.description:
• Definity Financial Corporation (TSX:DFY:CA) said on Thursday that catastrophe losses in Q3 would negatively impact operating income by approximately $170M net of reinsurance recoveries, or approximately $1.09 per common share, net of taxes and reinsurance.
  • Notable events in the quarter included a significant hailstorm in Alberta, severe rainstorms that led to flooding in Ontario and Quebec, and the Jasper wildfire. As a result of these events, the company has fully utilized its catastrophe aggregate reinsurance treaty limit for 2024.
  • In addition to the above, the company incurred $4 million of Sonnet Alberta auto catastrophe losses, which will be reported within exited lines.

data.title: Aura announces Q3 2024 production results
data.description:
• Aura Minerals (TSX:ORA:CA) on Thursday said its total production in Q3 2024 reached 68,246 gold equivalent ounces ((“GEO”)), 10% above the second quarter of 2024 and 10% above the same period last year at constant metal prices.
  • For the nine months of 2024, Aura’s total production was 200,758 GEO at current prices, on track to meet the 2024 Production Guidance of 244,000 – 292,000 GEO.
  • ORAAF closed -5.65% at $11.0102.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Intact Financial Corporation provides updated catastrophe loss estimate for Q3
data.description:
• Intact Financial Corporation (TSX:IFC:CA) on Thursday provided an update regarding catastrophe events in the third quarter of 2024, and said it estimates that total catastrophe losses for the quarter were approximately $1.2B on a pre-tax basis.
  • This includes the previously disclosed estimate of $1.1 billion in weather-related losses provided on August 21, in relation to 4 severe events in Canada.
  • The increase in the estimate reflects additional events in September which occurred mainly in the UK&I and US, including Hurricane Helene, Storm Boris as well as heavy rains in the UK.

data.title: Aptar authorizes $500M share repurchase
data.description:
• AptarGroup (NYSE:ATR) said on Thursday that its board has authorized the repurchase of up to $500M of the company’s common stock, replacing all previous authorizations.
  • The Board also declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.45 per share. The payment date is November 14, 2024, to stockholders of record as of October 24, 2024.

data.title: MSC Industrial Supply declares increase in regular quarterly dividend
data.description:
• MSC Industrial Supply (NYSE:MSM) on Thursday announced that its board of directors has declared a cash dividend of $0.85 per share, an increase from the previous regular quarterly dividend of $0.83 per share.
  • The $0.85 dividend is payable on November 27, 2024 to shareholders of record at the close of business on November 13, 2024.
  • MSM -0.0% after hours to $81.3.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SilverCrest Metals provides operational results for Q3
data.description:
• SilverCrest Metals (NYSE:SILV) on Thursday provided interim operational results for the third quarter of 2024 from its Las Chispas operation located in Sonora, Mexico.
  • The company recovered 14,928 ounces gold and 1.41 million ounces silver, and sold 15,204 ounces gold and 1.45 million ounces silver.
  • Generated record revenue of $80.4 million, a 10% increase from Q2 2024.
  • Average realized prices of $2,472/oz gold and $29.48/oz silver, increases of 11% and 6% respectively from Q2 2024.

data.title: NIKE names Tom Peddie as VP, GM of North America Geography
data.description:
• NIKE (NYSE:NKE) has announced that Tom Peddie will become Vice President, General Manager of the North America Geography, succeeding Scott Uzzell, who has decided to leave Nike.
  • Peddie will lead all aspects of Nike’s North America Geography, including Direct, Sales, Marketing, Digital, Consumer Construct and Territories, among others.
  • Peddie’s role will be effective Oct. 21. Nike will announce the backfill for VP, Marketplace Partners soon.

data.title: 7 children given bluebird's Skysona developed blood cancer: study
data.description:
Seven out of 67 children who were given bluebird bio’s (NASDAQ:BLUE) gene therapy Skysona during clinical trials ended up developing blood cancer, according to a new study.

Six of the children who received the treatment, which is for the rare neurodegenerative disease adrenoleukodystrophy, were later diagnosed with myelodysplastic syndrome, or MDS. A seventh child developed acute myeloid leukemia, or AML, after treatment, according to a study published Wednesday on The New England Journal of Medicine’s website.

Six of the pediatric patients subsequently underwent stem cell transplants, with one later dying from presumed graft-versus-host-disease. A seventh patient is awaiting a stem cell transplant, the study added.

The FDA granted accelerated approval to Skysona, also known as eli-cel, in September 2022 to slow the progression of neurologic dysfunction in boys aged four to 17 years with early, active cerebral adrenoleukodystrophy.

Skysona’s label already carries a warning that the gene therapy may turn on cancer-causing genes that could result in blood cancer.

The FDA approved bluebird’s gene therapy for sickle cell disease, Lyfgenia, last year. The product was approved the same day as a rival gene therapy for the disease called Casgevy, which was developed by Vertex Pharmaceuticals (VRTX) and CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP).

In late September, bluebird announced it was cutting its workforce by 25% as part of a restructuring initiative aimed at optimizing its cash structure and positioning the company to attract additional capital to extend its cash runway, according to a statement.
